title: Dynamic Rendering Need, Browser Contexts, Network Budgets, Readiness, and Cleanup · Behavior Case: Isolated Browser Context
summary: Every page launches a browser, waits for networkidle forever, loads third-party resources, shares cookies, and extracts an unstable animation state. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the isolated browser context increment in authorized bounded Playwright render adapter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Use isolated nonpersistent contexts with explicit viewport, locale, timezone, permissions, service-worker, storage, and credential policy.
Compare server HTML, embedded data, public API, progressive enhancement, and browser output and render only when the stakeholder evidence requires it.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-isolated-browser-context — Use isolated nonpersistent contexts with explicit viewport, locale, timezone, permissions, service-worker, storage, and credential policy.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-dynamic-rendering-playwright-5
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-dynamic-rendering-playwright-5.
# Case 9036: a multilingual public-service site review.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: introduce a block, private field, dangerous destination, or spreadsheet formula and stop or reject it.
def worked_scraper_dynamic_rendering_playwright_5(candidate_pages: int = 20, rendered_pages: int = 6, server_html_pages: int = 14, network_requests: int = 54, leaked_contexts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "authorizationworked5",
        "url9036",
        "response5dekrow",
        "treeworked59036",
        "frontier9036worked5",
        "report5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if candidate_pages < 1 or rendered_pages < 0 or server_html_pages < 0:
        return False, candidate_pages, "render-count"
    if rendered_pages + server_html_pages != candidate_pages:
        return False, rendered_pages, "render-necessity"
    if network_requests < rendered_pages or network_requests > 200:
        return False, network_requests, "browser-network-budget"
    if leaked_contexts > 0:
        return False, leaked_contexts, "browser-context-leak"
    assert rendered_pages + server_html_pages == candidate_pages and leaked_contexts == 0
    return True, rendered_pages, "bounded-dynamic-rendering"

# Evidence: scraper-isolated-browser-context-1ledoh
# Competency: scraper-isolated-browser-context.
# Case 5758: an authorized nonprofit content audit.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget.
def evidence_scraper_isolated_browser_context_1ledoh(candidate_pages: int = 20, rendered_pages: int = 6, server_html_pages: int = 14, network_requests: int = 54, leaked_contexts: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1ledoh = "1ledoh-hodel1-5758"
    assert evidence_variant_1ledoh
    evidence_axes_1ledoh = (
        "authorization1ledoh",
        "url5758",
        "responsehodel1",
        "tree1ledoh5758",
        "frontier57581ledoh",
        "reporthodel15758",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1ledoh)) == 6
    if candidate_pages < 1 or rendered_pages < 0 or server_html_pages < 0:
        return False, candidate_pages, "render-count"
    if rendered_pages + server_html_pages != candidate_pages:
        return False, rendered_pages, "render-necessity"
    if network_requests < rendered_pages or network_requests > 200:
        return False, network_requests, "browser-network-budget"
    if leaked_contexts > 0:
        return False, leaked_contexts, "browser-context-leak"
    assert rendered_pages + server_html_pages == candidate_pages and leaked_contexts == 0
    return True, rendered_pages, "bounded-dynamic-rendering"

At Authorized crawling and Python Repair the Isolated Browser Context defect · Theory Isolated Browser…, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 6002 during a keyboard and screen-reader report review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dynamic rendering need and validate isolated browser contexts, network budgets, readiness, and cleanup; personal data must remain minimized and redacted; then change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. stimulus.title: Isolated Browser Context failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: One shared browser page is isolated enough for all crawl targets.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use isolated nonpersistent contexts with explicit viewport, locale, timezone, permissions, service-worker, storage, and credential policy.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Isolated Browser Context, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Isolated Browser Context artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the isolated browser context defect evidence boundary for Isolated Browser Context.

For repair the isolated browser context defect, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Isolated Browser Context for authorized bounded Playwright render adapter.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Isolated Browser Context model · Theory Isolated Browser…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Isolated Browser Context: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 6907 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate dynamic rendering need and validate isolated browser contexts, network budgets, readiness, and cleanup; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use isolated nonpersistent contexts with explicit viewport, locale, timezone, permissions, service-worker, storage, and credential policy.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Isolated Browser Context model · Theory Isolated Browser….

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use isolated nonpersistent contexts with explicit viewport, locale, timezone, permissions, service-worker, storage, and credential policy.
